strengths: Current Staff role at re:cinq explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP work—exactly the scarce skill the role requires
Proven full-stack mastery across 15+ years: frontend (React, Ember.js, iOS/tvOS), backend (Node.js microservices at scale), and DevOps (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab/CI infrastructure)
Demonstrates engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD adoption at RIDE Capital, multiple testing workshops, open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and trunk-based development practices
concerns: Nest.js not explicitly mentioned in resume despite being primary stack—Node.js expertise is strong, but NestJS-specific patterns and architectural approach not evidenced
Testing frameworks (Vitest, Jest, Playwright) not listed, though testing philosophy is evident; unclear if hands-on with the specific tools this role uses daily
Security, authentication, and authorization best practices not documented in resume—no mention of OAuth, JWT, permission models, or compliance work despite this being a required skill
response.justification: Bogdan significantly exceeds the role's seniority (Staff Engineer vs. Senior) and experience (15+ years vs. 6+ required) with a current position that directly addresses the job's rarest requirement: AI-agent infrastructure and MCP development. Full-stack credentials are rock-solid—backend at scale (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby; low-latency systems; microservices), proven frontend across React, Ember, and iOS, and DevOps depth (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab). His engineering philosophy (TDD, code craftsmanship, testing culture) aligns with the role's values. Resume gaps are documentation, not capability: Nest.js isn't mentioned but extensive Node.js foundation makes it a quick acquisition; testing-framework specifics absent despite clear testing expertise; security practices undetailed. The AI-agent work is unusual and valuable—most candidates lack this. Interview would quickly clarify testing-tool familiarity and security background.
